figures.caption: Fig. 5: Implementation of fundamental manipulation primitives. Red dots denote target spatial keypoints, and blue arrows indicate trajectory directions.
Table 4: Spatial intelligence benchmark of VLMs. Time: average seconds per task.
Table 1: Comparison with data-driven (TrajBooster, Ψ0) and VLM-driven (CaP) baselines on diverse tasks. Easy denotes in-domain tasks, while Hard denotes more complex out-of-distribution tasks.

Fig. 2: The overview of our humanoid whole-body manipulation framework. Our framework consists of two components: an Active Spatial Brain for active spatial perception, understanding and planing; and a Generalizable Action Cerebellum for executable action generation.
